name: GCT_OCCURRENCE_DEAD
false_form: Bürgisser-Ikenmeyer-Panova killed GCT entirely; occurrence obstructions for det/padded-perm are still a viable path to VP vs VNP separation.
true_form: BIP 2019 (J. AMS) killed *occurrence* obstructions for (det_m, padded_perm_{n,m}, m=poly(n)) specifically. Multiplicity, vanishing-ideal, outside-orbit, and equivariant obstructions remain. Substrate must reject any agent attempt to construct an OccurrenceObstruction in this regime as a sentinel-violation.

name: ZAUNER_FALSE_ANCHOR
false_form: Zauner's SIC-POVM conjecture was proved unconditionally in 2025.
true_form: Appleby-Flammia-Kopp 2025 (arXiv:2501.03970) gives a construction CONDITIONAL on the order-1 abelian Stark conjecture for real quadratic fields plus a Shintani-Faddeev modular cocycle special-value identity. Substrate must reject 'Zauner proved' without a conditional annotation naming both Stark and Shintani-Faddeev as upstream assumptions. Use full author names; never abbreviate to 'AFK' in canonical form.

name: HILLAR_LIM_SYMRANK_Q_RESOLVED
true_form: Symmetric-rank over Q is RESOLVED by Shitov 2016 ('How hard is the tensor rank?', arXiv:1611.01559). The proof reduces tensor rank over an integral domain to systems of polynomial equations, then specializes to symmetric tensors over Q. Reverse-direction false-anchor: substrate must NOT show this as open. Hillar-Lim NP-hardness over R remains the standard reference.
citation: arXiv:1611.01559 (Shitov 2016, 'How hard is the tensor rank?')
verification_source: Wave 1 Gemini Deep Research, prompt 01 — citation arXiv:1605.07532 was wrong (PDE paper); corrected to arXiv:1611.01559

name: SAXL_T99_FALSELY_RESOLVED
false_form: Saxl's conjecture (T#99) was solved unconditionally by Sellke 2025/26 (arXiv:2512.15035) or by Lee 2025.
true_form: Saxl conjecture is OPEN. Lee 2025 (arXiv:2512.15035) was WITHDRAWN within 3 days (2025-12-20) due to 'mathematical gaps identified by expert reviewers' — do not cite as proof. Luo-Sellke 2017 (J. Algebraic Combin.) proved only the FOURTH-POWER relaxation. A 2022 follow-on (centre-mersenne) tightened to the CUBE. The tensor square — the conjecture proper — remains open. Substrate must NOT propagate the false 'solved' claim. Forward false-anchor: an LLM may memorize Lee's withdrawn abstract since arXiv preprint scrapers often miss withdrawal notices.
citation: arXiv:2512.15035 (Lee 2025, WITHDRAWN); Luo-Sellke 2017 J. Algebraic Combin. (fourth power only)
verification_source: Wave 1 Gemini Deep Research, prompt 01 — INVERTED from prior 2026-05-10 entry which incorrectly claimed Saxl was solved. Wave 1 surfaced the Lee withdrawal and the Luo-Sellke fourth-power scope.

name: CACTUS_BARRIER_6M_MINUS_4
false_form: Determinantal / rank-method lower bounds on border rank R-bar(M<m>) for m x m x m matrix multiplication tensors can in principle exceed any polynomial in m.
true_form: Buczyński Feb 2026 (arXiv:2602.11309) establishes the cactus barrier: determinantal LBs on R-bar(M<m>) cannot exceed 6m - 4. Any P31 BorderRankWitness claiming r > 6m - 4 for an m x m x m tensor is auto-flagged for re-verification via P29 apolarity (NOT P31 flattenings).

name: LUCCA_ATTRIBUTION
false_form: Conjecture 16 of arXiv:2603.29571 was jointly proposed by Bandeira and Dmitriev.
true_form: Conjecture 16 was proposed by Lucca; Bandeira and Dmitriev are EDITORS of arXiv:2603.29571, not joint proposers. Authorship-vs-proposership distinction matters for citation hygiene.

name: TENSOR_TYPE2_NOT_SQRT_LOG_D
false_form: The type-2 constant for tensors scales as sqrt(log d), the same as the matrix Bernstein bound.
true_form: sqrt(log d) is the MATRIX-only constant. The TENSOR case scales as d^{1/2 - 1/p} polylog (BGJLR STOC 2025 for p >= 2r; p < 2r open behind a volumetric barrier). Cross-tier dimensional confusion; PATTERN_BASE_RATE_NEGLECT applies.

name: EQUIVARIANT_EXPONENTIAL_RESTRICTED
false_form: Landsberg-Ressayre 2017 gives an exponential lower bound on dc(perm_n) unrestricted.
true_form: Landsberg-Ressayre 2017 (arXiv:1508.05788) exponential lower bound on dc(perm) is RESTRICTED to the equivariant model only. It is NOT a lower bound on dc(perm_n) unrestricted. Mignon-Ressayre n^2/2 (2004 IMRN) remains the current best for the unrestricted dc(perm_n). HARD-5 / PATTERN_RANK_PARITY_LEAK at the model-restriction layer.

name: BORDER_CACTUS_DISTINCT_FIFTH_RANK
false_form: Border cactus rank cr-bar is a synonym for cactus rank cr or border rank R-bar.
true_form: Border cactus rank cr-bar is a DISTINCT FIFTH rank invariant (Buczyńska-Buczyński Jan 2026, arXiv:2601.19558). Substrate must track at least five rank coordinates (R, R-bar, sr, cr, cr-bar) and never collapse them. RankZooSignature primitive (Tier-A++) enforces this.

name: TYPE2_FIVE_REGION_RARE
false_form: The type-2 tensor constant in T#72 has the standard textbook regime structure of matrix Bernstein.
true_form: Type-2 tensor constant has unusual five-region simultaneity (regimes: matrix_r2, p_geq_2r, p_lt_2r, p_eq_infty, plus a fifth boundary regime). Five-application convergence is RARE; Learner trained on textbook matrix Bernstein hallucinates sqrt(log d) for tensors. Tagged PATTERN_BASE_RATE_NEGLECT.

name: SAXL_CUBE_ANCHOR
false_form: The tensor cube variant of the Saxl conjecture remains open.
true_form: The tensor CUBE variant (S_{ρ_n})^⊗3 was proven to contain all irreducible representations of S_N in 2022 (centre-mersenne follow-on to Luo-Sellke 2017). The Saxl conjecture proper (tensor SQUARE) remains open. Companion entry to AA-004; surfaces the precise gap that remains.
citation: centre-mersenne 2022 follow-on; Luo-Sellke 2017 J. Algebraic Combin.
verification_source: Wave 1 Gemini Deep Research, prompt 01 — newly discovered sub-anchor

name: TENSOR_RANK_Z_UNDECIDABLE
false_form: Tensor rank over Z is computable in exponential time.
true_form: Tensor rank over Z is mathematically UNDECIDABLE (Shitov 2016, arXiv:1611.01559). This answers the 1980 Gonzalez-Ja'Ja' conjecture. The reduction goes through Hilbert's 10th problem via the equivalence between tensor rank over an integral domain and systems of polynomial equations over that domain.
citation: arXiv:1611.01559 (Shitov 2016, 'How hard is the tensor rank?')
verification_source: Wave 1 Gemini Deep Research, prompt 01 — newly discovered sub-anchor
